data.content:
HANDOFF CAPSULE (foolery-f8ae shipment)

## Scope change rationale

Original body asked for a unit test that the watchdog emits
watchdog_fired. That test already shipped via foolery-2782
(src/lib/__tests__/agent-session-runtime-watchdog-fired.test.ts).
Duplicating it would be make-work. Scope was repurposed to the
end-to-end observability canary that would have caught the 557d
silent-SIGTERM bug.

## What this test locks

File: src/lib/__tests__/terminal-manager-watchdog-e2e.test.ts
Commits: 144f4547 (initial), f781a08e (062f gate flip)

Exercises real createSessionRuntime + wireTakeChildClose with a
mock ChildProcess and fake timers, then locks seven discrete,
named assertions covering the FULL chain a 557d-style silent
kill must leave visible:

 1. watchdog_fired lifecycle event (timeoutMs, msSinceLastEvent).
    Breaks the unit-level invariant from foolery-2782.
 2. console.warn("[terminal-manager] [watchdog] timeout_fired
    pid=77777 timeoutMs=50 ... reason=timeout"). Breaks if the
    watchdog is silenced.
 3. process.kill(-pid, "SIGTERM") invoked. Breaks if
    terminateProcessGroup no longer SIGTERMs the process group.
 4. [watchdog] warn ordered BEFORE the SIGTERM. Breaks if the
    cause log is moved after the effect.
 5. console.warn("[terminal-manager] [terminate-process-group]
    ... reason=watchdog_timeout") (from foolery-062f,
    commit 8929ae99). Breaks if 062f's terminate log is dropped
    or its reason tag is regressed.
 6. Enriched child_close console.log (from foolery-e750) with
    signal=SIGTERM, exitReason=timeout, msSinceLastStdout=<num>,
    lastEventType=null. Breaks if e750's diagnostic enrichment
    is stripped.
 7. recordTakeLoopLifecycle child_close payload carrying the
    same childSignal / childExitCode / exitReason /
    msSinceLastStdout / lastEventType fields so the interaction
    log chain stays intact.

## Future-agent warning

If you are about to silence, rename, or drop any of the log
strings named above, this test will fail with a specific named
message. THE CORRECT RESPONSE IS TO FIX THE LOGGING, NOT THE
TEST. Each of these log lines was paid for with a 557d-style
production incident. Keep them.

## Canary verified end-to-end

Mutation probed both the [watchdog] warn and the enriched
child_close log during implementation. Each mutation produced
specific named failures in 2 of the 7 tests; restoring the
original code restored the green state.

## Reference

Original bug: foolery-557d (silent SIGTERM visibility gap).
Related shipped knots: foolery-2782 (watchdog log + unit test),
foolery-e750 (child_close enrichment), foolery-062f
(terminateProcessGroup log).
